name: tundeep
title: tundeep

commands.name: tundeep
commands.usage: tundeep <-i iface|[-t|-T] tapiface> <-h ip> <-p port> [-6] [-C] <-c|-s> [-x tapip] [-y tapmask] [-u tapmac] [-b bpf] [-d udp mode] [-e udp remote] [-K]

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# tundeep -h
 tundeep: option requires an argument -- 'h'
 Option -h error.
 *** tundeep v1.0_20170728 by Adam Palmer <adam@adampalmer.me> ***
 Usage: tundeep <-i iface|[-t|-T] tapiface> <-h ip> <-p port> [-6] [-C] <-c|-s> [-x tapip] [-y tapmask] [-u tapmac] [-b bpf] [-d udp mode] [-e udp remote] [-K]
 
 -6 IPv6 mode
 -C compress mode
 -K disable checksum
 -a print all pcap devs
 -b "bpf"
 -i interface to bind to
 -h IP to bind to/connect to
 -p port to bind to/connect to
 -c client mode
 -s server mode
 -d udp mode
 -e udp peer
 -t tap interface 
 -T ipv6 tap interface 
 -u tap mac 
 -x if -t mode, set iface ip, if -T mode, set iface ipv6 ip
 -y if -t mode, set iface mask, if -T mode, set iface ipv6 prefixlen
 --------------------

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=a64321ee1af13ae6b72d74524c828314b1728816, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Layer 2 VPN/injection tool
The tool resides [almost] entirely in user space on the victim aside from the
pcap requirement.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use tundeep because ### tundeep.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `tundeep <-i iface|[-t|-T] tapiface> <-h ip> <-p port> [-6] [-C] <-c|-s> [-x tapip] [-y tapmask] [-u tapmac] [-b bpf] [-d udp mode] [-e udp remote] [-K]` as documented upstream for tundeep.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair tundeep with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `tundeep --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: tundeep <-i iface|[-t|-T] tapiface> <-h ip> <-p port> [-6] [-C] <-c|-s> [-x tapip] [-y tapmask] [-u tapmac] [-b bpf] [-d udp mode] [-e udp remote] [-K]

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install tundeep. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `tundeep <-i iface|[-t|-T] tapiface> <-h ip> <-p port> [-6] [-C] <-c|-s> [-x tapip] [-y tapmask] [-u tapmac] [-b bpf] [-d udp mode] [-e udp remote] [-K]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### tundeep.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install tundeep
